chemistry.reaction_name: Photosensitized HO2 radical formation from imidazole-2-carboxaldehyde
chemistry.mechanism_type: Triplet state photosensitization with H-atom transfer
chemistry.scope_summary: Photosensitized formation of gas-phase HO2 radicals from IC triplet states via H-atom transfer from citric acid matrix, with catalytic regeneration of IC through reaction with O2. System studied as function of light intensity, humidity (25-63% RH), O2 concentration, and IC loading.

optimization_table.key_findings: HO2 production exhibited linear dependence on actinic flux and IC × CA concentration product

light_source.position_relative_to_reactor: surrounding the flow tube in a circular arrangement

figures.flow_path: Gas flows through humidifier, then through coated-wall flow tube with lamps, with exhaust to analytical instruments
figures.key_information: Two different experimental setups (a) and (b) shown with coated-wall flow tube reactor, water-cooled housing, and multiple gas inlets with humidity control (~0% RH)
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Sketch of the photochemical flow tube reactor setups at PSI for  (a)  Setup 1 in 2013 measuring NO 2  generation and  (b)  for Setup 2 in 2014 measuring NO loss.

Figure 8.  Proposed mechanism, modified and expanded to photosensitization of IC based on Canonica et al. (1995), George et al. (2005) and Aregahegn et al. (2013). The reaction in the white square represents the gas-phase, and the blue square represents the aqueous phase. DH is an H donor (e.g., CA, another IC, H 2 O  +  CA matrix to be determined from flash photolysis).

translation_logic.flow_advantage: Enables controlled gas-phase reaction conditions with precise residence time and continuous operation for studying atmospheric processes
translation_logic.what_changed: Study focused on atmospheric photochemistry rather than synthetic chemistry
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Enables study of gas-phase radical chemistry under controlled conditions

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing coated-wall flow tube setup with multiple gas inlets and analytical instruments
Mechanistic scheme showing photosensitized radical formation pathway

notes: This is an atmospheric photochemistry study rather than a synthetic chemistry flow vs batch comparison. The reactor scheme figure provides detailed setup information but quantitative parameters are limited. No optimization table or batch baseline present as this is fundamental atmospheric research.
